% Auto-generated by scripts/build_biegrid_four_tables.py
\section*{BIEGrid Benchmarks (up to $N=321{,}489$)}
All runs use square BIEGrid in double precision with dense assembly, $\kappa=0$, max tree depth $10$, one right-hand side, GMRES tolerance $10^{-10}$, 4 threads, and sample caching disabled (\texttt{save\_samples=false}, \texttt{load\_samples=false}).

\subsection*{PerLevel, $k=p=40$}
\begin{table}[h]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{r r r r r r r}
\hline
$N$ & $\mathrm{cond}(\mathbf{A})$ & relerr & errsolve & $n_{\mathrm{samples}}$ & no prec $n_{\mathrm{iter}}$ & prec $n_{\mathrm{iter}}$ \\
\hline
80,656 & 1.4e+05 & 2.8e-06 & 1.0e-01 & 1,640 & 4,157 & 6 \\
160,801 & 2.8e+05 & 8.8e-06 & 5.5e-01 & 1,640 & 5,000 & 11 \\
321,489 & 7.5e+05 & 2.8e-05 & 4.2e+00 & 1,640 & 5,000 & 20 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
\caption{BIEGrid summary for PerLevel sampling mode with $k=p=40$.}
\label{tab:biegrid_perlevel_k40}
\end{table}
